subject: Re: Batch pre-processing in Warp and white lines across thon rings
body:
Hi Cyan,

I can’t see your attachments but two things come to mind

1) for the ‘artificial cut’ there has been some discussion about warp mishandling some mrc file headers, running the files through newstack (from IMOD) or similar has fixed this issue for people

2) for handling tomograms in batch, warp expects movies for each tilt image to all be in the same directory, regardless of which tomogram they come from - it looks at mdoc files to see which images are part of the same tilt series

Your filenames look like they were produced by Tomo5, if you’re using older data and don’t have mdoc files then https://github.com/alisterburt/mdocspoofer <https://github.com/alisterburt/mdocspoofer> can be used to generate ‘fake’ mdocs for warp processing.

Cheers and good luck!

Alister

> On 6 Dec 2021, at 11:45, Cyan Ching <xingyichengcyanching@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Dear Dimitry,
> 
> Good morning!
> 
> Is there perhaps a way to batch pre-process tens of tomograms of one acquisition using Warp? Does Warp require special file naming to distinguish frames of different tomograms? 
> 
> Following Warp-based pre-processing (motion and CTF correction) of a few tomograms (in mrc format, acquired on a TF Glacios 200kV cryo-transmission electron microscopes (Cryo-TEM) equipped with Falcon 3 direct electron detectors), white lines appeared across thon rings in the Fourier space and individual tilts in real space seemed artificially cut as it is shown in the attached images. Each movie of the frames is named as: Position_1_001[-9.00]_Fractions.mrc. Is there an explanation for this? Do you have any suggestions on how this could be fixed?
> 
> Thank you so much for your help in advance!
> 
> Wish you a great day!
> 
> Best,
> 
> Cyan Ching
> 
> PhD student at the Physical Chemistry Curie Lab
> 
>
